name: Finnish Butter Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: TASTE OF HOME, DEC/JAN 1996
1. In a mixing bowl, cream butter and sugar until smooth.
2. Stir in almond extract.
3. Gradually add 1-1/4 cups flour; mix well with a spoon.
4. Knead the remaining flour into the dough until it is no longer sticky.
5. Cover the dough and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
6. Roll out the chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of 1/4 inch (0.25").
7. Cut shapes with a small cookie cutter (1-1/2 to 2 inches) using an etched template.
8. Place the cut cookies onto ungreased baking sheets, spacing them about 1 inch apart.
9. Beat egg white until it becomes foamy and glossy.
10. Brush the egg white over each cookie using a pastry brush or spoon.
11. Sprinkle sugar and ground almonds evenly over the tops of the cookies.
12. Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 7-8 minutes, or until lightly browned and set.
13. Cool completely on wire racks.
Yield: Approximately 4 dozen cookies.
